channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
item.description: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain
item.description: Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
item.description: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Min...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Bahrain Alert App Deploys Android Surveillance Malware
item.description: A malicious application delivers four-stage Android spyware via phony Google Play sites, exploiting civilian fear during Iranian missile strikes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
item.description: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Swiss rail giant Stadler rejects $12.3M ransom demand after cyberattack
item.description: Swiss rail vehicle manufacturer Stadler Rail says the Everest ransomware gang demanded about $12.3 million after breaching a data exchange platform shared with one of its suppliers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: When AI Attacks: OpenAI Models Autonomously Hack Hugging Face
item.description: Advanced LLMs escaped their sandboxes while attempting to achieve a non-malicious benchmark test objective....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it
item.description: Enterprise AI can accelerate ransomware attacks when AI assistants and agents inherit excessive permissions or compromised identities. Acronis explains how identity controls, governance, and least-pri...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a sil...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New InfraTrust report reveals infrastructure flaws admins should patch first
item.description: Eclypsium has launched InfraTrust, a new infrastructure cybersecurity knowledge base and monthly InfraTrust Pulse report designed to help organizations prioritize vulnerabilities affecting infrastruct...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats
item.description: The Adobe Acrobat extension for Chrome could be used to access conversations and data rendered in WhatsApp Web without any form of authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
item.description:
A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS sc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security
item.description: Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need, empl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders urgent action on actively exploited Langflow RCE flaw
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday ordered U.S. government agencies to prioritize patching an actively exploited vulnerability in the Langflow visual framework for ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugg...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: EU Financial Institutions Leak Data Through Cookie Trackers
item.description: European and US banks inadvertently transmitted customer data to ad platforms via tracking pixels, raising serious compliance, security, and privacy concerns....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Why Modern SOCs Need Multi-Layered Detections
item.description: The cycle is over. For years, cybersecurity followed a familiar pattern: defenses improved, attackers adapted, and the back-and-forth continued. Today, AI-equipped attackers are simply outpacing defen...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stop renting storage space — this lifetime 2TB plan is yours for $59
item.description: Cloud storage costs tend to creep up over time, since most services charge monthly or annually for as long as you use them. FileJump's Lifetime Plan skips that model entirely, offering 2TB of cloud st...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October
item.description: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
item.description: American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
item.description: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authori...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live ga...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing
item.description: OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description: A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Police dismantle Kratos phishing platform, arrest developer
item.description: Authorities in Germany and the U.S. dismantled the central infrastructure of Kratos, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform with global reach, and its developer was arrested in Indonesia. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FakeGit campaign uses 7,600 GitHub repos to push SmartLoader malware
item.description: A large-scale operation dubbed 'FakeGit' is pushing SmartLoader and StealC malware through 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories that accumulated more than 14 million downloads. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, but It's Not Because of AI
item.description: Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Using LLMs to Find and Prioritize Vulnerabilities Is No Easy Task
item.description: The latest large language models have high false-positive rates and fail to take into account the context of scans, leading to more work for AppSec professionals....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
